description_long: This object consists of a cluster of several chrysanthemum-like flowers with long, thin, radiating petals in shades of pink, purple, and cream. The flowers are supported by slender green stems, some of which branch off to hold multiple blooms at varying heights. The petals are densely packed and appear spiky, giving the flowers a textured, almost feathery appearance. Small, ovate green leaves are attached intermittently along the stems, adding contrast to the vibrant flower heads. The overall arrangement is naturalistic, resembling a wild or garden bouquet, with flowers oriented in different directions. The coloration and structure suggest a decorative plant commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or landscaped sidewalks.
description: A cluster of spiky, chrysanthemum-like flowers in pink, purple, and cream, with slender green stems and small ovate leaves. The flowers are densely petaled and arranged at varying heights, creating a natural bouquet effect.
description_view_0: Several spiky flowers are visible, with the tallest pink bloom standing out and smaller flowers clustered below, showing a mix of pink, purple, and cream hues.
description_view_1: The bouquet appears more spread out, with the flowers fanning to the left and the stems and leaves more visible, highlighting the layered arrangement.
description_view_2: The largest pink flower dominates the right side, while a prominent purple flower is visible in the foreground, with the stems curving gracefully.
description_view_3: The view is dominated by the largest pink flower, with cream and brownish flowers partially obscured behind it, and fewer leaves visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, radial pattern around the center of each flower head, with significant overlapping and layering, creating a spiky, spherical appearance typical of composite inflorescences.
